verdict: Proposal approved. Revision 2 resolves PO-B-01 (the sole blocker from the prior conditional approval) by adding a mandatory Phase 0: Baseline Metrics Recording with explicit recording steps, concrete tools/data sources, a gate condition preventing Phase 1 start, and a git commit as proof of baseline recording. The Section 7.1 rollout diagram and 7.3 effort estimates are updated accordingly. Section 11 catalogues all 30 non-blocking issues and 26 suggestions from Run 2 reviewers as implementation guidance with 8 recurring themes. No new blocking issues introduced. The proposal is ready for implementation.
summary: Revision 2 of the Comprehensive UX Audit and Remediation proposal addresses the single remaining blocker from the prior 9.0/10 conditional approval. Phase 0 now includes 4 explicit recording steps (crash-free rate from Xcode Organizer, support ticket count, CI pass rate, commit as proof) with a hard gate on Phase 1 start. The 8-phase plan (Phase 0 through Phase 7) estimates 24.5-33.5 hours to remediate 11 error handling defects, 19 accessibility findings, 3 navigation edge cases, 5 visual consistency issues, and 2 in-code TODOs. Key strengths: verified code-level audit data with false positives removed, additive ServiceResult migration strategy eliminating breaking changes, service-layer error broadcasting for deduplication, Phase 3.5 validation spike as early checkpoint, MVP cut-line at hour 14 with defined scope reduction, 15 manual test scenarios with explicit pass criteria, user-outcome success criteria, VoiceOver audit checklist, per-phase rollback plans, and incremental 6-PR merge strategy. Section 11 comprehensively incorporates all prior reviewer feedback (30 non-blocking issues, 26 suggestions, 8 recurring themes) as implementation-phase reference material.

non_blocking_issues.title: Effort estimate variance remains wide at 37%
non_blocking_issues.description: The total effort range of 24.5-33.5 hours represents a 37% variance (9-hour spread). While the MVP cut-line at ~14 hours mitigates overrun risk, the wide range reflects uncertainty in Phase 4 (6-7h) and Phase 5 (5-7h). After Phase 3.5 provides concrete velocity data, estimates should be tightened.
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: After Phase 3.5, re-estimate remaining phases using actual velocity from Phases 0-3. If cumulative actual exceeds the upper estimate by more than 20%, activate the MVP cut-line proactively rather than waiting for the hour-14 trigger.

non_blocking_issues.title: CoalescedErrorBannerView should default to MVP fallback posture
non_blocking_issues.description: CoalescedErrorBannerView (Phase 4.14) is the highest-complexity single deliverable in the proposal. The MVP fallback (stacked ErrorBannerViews) is defined but the implementation plan assumes the full component unless explicitly cut. The multi-error dashboard scenario is an edge case for power users, not a core P0 silent-failure defect. Defaulting to the MVP fallback reduces Phase 4 estimate by ~2 hours and eliminates the riskiest single deliverable.
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Already tracked as PO-NB-01 and recurring theme #1 in Section 11. Implementers should default to stacked ErrorBannerView MVP. Only build the full CoalescedErrorBannerView if Phase 3.5 demo explicitly validates the need for collapsed/expanded interaction.

non_blocking_issues.title: Phase 3.5 iteration budget could trigger premature MVP cut-line
non_blocking_issues.description: Phase 3.5 includes a stakeholder demo gate. If the demo requires one iteration cycle (2-3 hours), cumulative hours may reach 14-17, triggering the MVP cut-line. This could prematurely lose Phase 4 view integration scope that is essential to user-visible improvement.
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Already tracked as PO-NB-03 in Section 11. Allow up to 2 iterations within Phase 3.5 before evaluating the MVP cut-line. Consider adjusting the trigger from hour 14 to hour 16 to absorb one demo iteration cycle.

non_blocking_issues.title: Manual test matrix gates underspecified for Phase 5 and Phase 6 PRs
non_blocking_issues.description: Section 8.2 gates manual testing on the Phase 4 PR merge. Phase 5 (accessibility) and Phase 6 (navigation) have their own test scenarios (5, 6, 10 for Phase 5; scenario 7 for Phase 6) that need independent sign-off.
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Already tracked as PO-NB-04 in Section 11. Implementers should treat each PR's relevant test scenarios as merge-blocking for that specific PR.

non_blocking_issues.title: Onboarding font remediation (45 instances) lacks quantitative visual regression pass/fail criteria
non_blocking_issues.description: Phase 5.7 replaces 45 hardcoded font sizes in onboarding -- the largest single batch change. Phase 7.9 mentions visual regression check but does not define quantitative pass/fail. Onboarding is the first-run experience; visual regression here has outsized impact.
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Already tracked as PO-NB-07 in Section 11. Define: rendered size difference > 10% at default text size = regression. Require before/after screenshots attached to the Phase 5 PR.

non_blocking_issues.title: No production data validates P0 severity ranking for silent failures
non_blocking_issues.description: P0 severity for silent failures (ERR-01 through ERR-11) is based on code-level impact analysis, which is sound but not validated by production crash reports, support tickets, or user analytics. A single data point would strengthen the business case.
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Already tracked as PO-NB-06 in Section 11. Accept code-level severity as best available signal. Phase 0 baseline recording will capture the first production data points (support tickets). Post-ship monitoring validates priorities for future work.

suggestions.title: Add a lightweight implementation progress tracker
suggestions.description: With 8 phases and 6 PRs, a simple 'Phase | Estimated | Actual | Cumulative' table updated after each phase would help the implementer make timely scope decisions against the 32-hour budget and MVP cut-line.

suggestions.title: Sequence Phase 1 quick wins first
suggestions.description: Steps 1.7 (deprecation marker), 1.10 (color token swap), and 1.2 (ChartError bridge) are very low-risk and fast. Sequencing these before the heavier Steps 1.1 (ErrorTranslator 25 cases) and 1.4 (AsyncContentView upgrade) provides early momentum.

suggestions.title: Define Phase 3.5 demo script with specific scenarios
suggestions.description: Phase 3.5.3 says 'Demo to product stakeholder' without a script. Providing 3-4 specific walkthrough scenarios (e.g., kill network on Dashboard, show GoalDetail with stale cache, retry and observe transition, trigger multi-error state) makes the demo repeatable and the sign-off criteria objective.

suggestions.title: Frame new error banners positively in user communication
suggestions.description: Users will see new error banners where they previously saw clean (but silently broken) screens. Consider a one-time tooltip or What's New note: 'We now show you when data may be outdated, so you always know what you're seeing.' This prevents users from perceiving the fix as a regression.

assumptions: The 25-case AppError enum and 6-case ChartError enum are complete and verified. No additional error cases will be added during implementation that would expand ErrorTranslator scope.
The existing 4 ErrorAwareViewModel-conforming ViewModels are correctly implemented and serve as reliable patterns for the remaining 5 ViewModels.
The additive ServiceResult migration strategy (new *Result methods alongside existing async throws methods) will not cause protocol conformance issues in test doubles or mock implementations.
The 77 hardcoded .system(size:) instances across 22 files is an accurate count verified against source.
The Phase 3.5 validation spike on 2 screens (Dashboard, GoalDetail) is sufficient to validate the error contract before full view-layer rollout.
The 32-hour budget is for a single developer, and the MVP cut-line provides adequate fallback if the budget is exceeded.
DIContainer supports registration of FreshnessPolicy and can inject it into services without architectural changes.
CloudKit persistence does not introduce error modes beyond those documented in the 25-case AppError enum.
Xcode Organizer crash-free rate data (or TestFlight equivalent) is accessible for Phase 0 baseline recording.
Stakeholder availability for Phase 3.5 demo will not create a multi-day blocking delay.
The existing EmptyStateView factory pattern can accommodate the new .onboardingFailure case without architectural changes.
ErrorHandler.shared can be safely bypassed in conforming ViewModels without breaking fire-and-forget error paths (widget updates, notifications).
